level_signals: 8+ years of experience in program management, operations, or support engineering
Role involves leading strategy and program design across multiple initiatives
Expected to help shape vision and strategy of a new team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 8+ years of experience in program management, operations, or support engineering, with a focus on building scalable processes and tooling
Have shipped or maintained tools and automations (dashboards, ETL pipelines, low-code apps) that eliminated manual work and scaled beyond a single team
Treat ChatGPT & LLMs as default co-developers, rapidly turning natural-language ideas into working code or queries
Deeply enjoy working cross-functionally and are skilled at building relationships with Product, Engineering, and Operations teams
Excel in a dynamic, high-impact team environment where you can create structure and make meaningful contributions
Are passionate about using tools and automation to drive efficiency and improve customer outcomes
Possess exceptional communication, organization, and problem-solving skills
Have a high ownership mindset with a bias toward action; comfortable operating in ambiguity, driving clarity, and rolling up their sleeves to get things done

scope.surface: Senior Support Engineering team within User Operations; Tier 3 frontline support delivery for strategic customers
scope.business_model: B2B and B2C AI product deployment (enterprise and startup customers)
primary_metrics_inferred: Reduction in manual work through automation
Productivity and workflow improvements for support team
core_responsibilities: Lead support delivery programs for Tier 3 frontline delivery for our most strategic customers, including productivity and workflow improvements, team operations, and knowledge management for the Support Delivery team

Partner with Support Delivery and cross-functional leadership to define the experience, stand up the program, manage pilots and rollout, and ensure premium operations and playbooks stay healthy over time
Lead strategy and program design for internal support initiatives, including specialization programs, quality initiatives, community support, product reliability and launch supportability
Coordinate requirements, implementation, and ongoing iteration with internal build owners and systems partners
Lead projects to identify, implement, and scale tools and automation that improve customer support efficiency and effectiveness

Identify and scale practical AI-powered workflows across support operations, using automation, LLMs, and internal tooling to reduce manual work, improve resolution quality, and help the team operate more effectively at scale
Collaborate across teams to identify opportunities for improvement, reduce friction, and enhance overall customer satisfaction
Help shape the vision and strategy of a new team, contributing to its long-term success and influence across the organization

green_flags: Clear hybrid policy stated (3 days in-office per week)
Role explicitly describes foundational impact — building new team and defining strategy
Intersection of technical (ETL, LLMs, automation) and operational skills is well-defined
OpenAI's scale offers meaningful scope and influence
red_flags: San Francisco onsite requirement (3 days/week) limits remote candidates
Role requires 8+ years but JD language is aspirational and vague in several places, making exact expectations harder to gauge

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement and strategic scope of role)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer types mentioned)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
posting_first_seen and posting_last_updated (not available in source data)
ambiguities: Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; classified as 'unclear'

The JD lists 'You might thrive in this role if you:' as the header for requirements — this phrasing is softer than 'Required', but all items under it read as hard requirements given the seniority and specificity; classified as must_haves with severity hard
No nice-to-haves or preferred qualifications section exists in the JD; nice_to_haves left empty
